programmes.show_description: Celebrating Arctic Monkeys' Whatever People Say I Am... with Richard Hawley and Lava La Rue!
Three hours of musical curiosities from Mark Lamarr's record box including past sessions from Graham Coxon and Elvis Costello.
Hope of the States play live in Bristol in 2003, and there's BBC sessions from Charlotte Adigery and Orquesta La Clave.
Want to hear the latest tracks 6 Music have added to their playlist? Then listen here!
Names in Songs returns. Can you hear your name in a well-known song?
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Actor Cillian Murphy joins around new film, Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man.
Craig celebrates your midweek wins for Wednesday, I'm In Love, and we hear another of your left-field music suggestions as we turn Left At The Lights.
TOMORA, the new duo of AURORA and Tom Rowlands of The Chemical Brothers join Huw, and we get to know the band Ugly Ozo in this week's 'Huw Are You'.
Nathan chats to Birmingham's Tony Bontana about the making of his latest album, My Name. Tony Bontana is a rapper and producer who has put out 35 albums to date.
Gideon Coe plays his music loves from every era and genre. Will include Honey, Hairbands, Ships and a Ship - come in and enjoy.
Kelly Lee Owens picks some of her Favourite Film Scores.
The members of legendary cult band Cardiacs pick an hour of music on the theme of life-affirming, death-accepting.
10/7. Alexis Korner presents an in-depth documentary of the Rolling Stones' 1975 tour of America, talking to organisers and promoters, as well as band members and fans.
Alexis Korner continues his series on influential guitarists with a profile of the Who's Pete Townshend. First broadcast in 1983.
There's an early Queen session from 1973, The Icicle Works in concert, and BBC sessions from Public Image Ltd and Sebastien Tellier!
Soundtrack the early hours with back-to-back music, plus exclusive BBC sessions.
Chris Hawkins fires up Thursday with the biggest tunes and future classics.
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Lauren's got the finest music to motivate your Thursday, with the best tunes old and new.
Chloe from Lime Garden talks about their new album 'Maybe Not Tonight', and we boil the Kettle of Metal.
Huw has handpicked music, old and new, plus we round up the gigs, shows and festivals that everybody is talking about in today's Gig Lisztomania.
Reviewing this week's new releases.
Nathan Shepherd and Deb Grant hand-pick and share their latest new music obsessions.
Gideon Coe plays his music loves from every era and genre.
A deep dive into the mind of an artist.
